operating_state: Total revenue increased $3.8 billion to $24.2 billion driven by organic base fee growth of 9% from record net inflows of $698 billion, market appreciation impact on average AUM, and acquisitions including GIP, HPS, and Preqin. Investment advisory fees rose while performance fees increased primarily from private markets. ETF net inflows reached $527 billion with strong performance across strategies, while private markets generated substantial inflows. Technology services revenue grew driven by sustained Aladdin demand and Preqin acquisition contribution.
cost_structure: Total expenses increased $4.3 billion to $17.2 billion primarily from acquisitions, compressing operating margin to 29.1% GAAP and 44.1% as adjusted. Employee compensation rose including HPS retention awards and higher incentive compensation from increased operating income. Amortization expense increased significantly driven by HPS and Preqin intangible assets, while general administration expense rose including charitable contribution and restructuring charges.
material_events: Completed acquisitions of Preqin for $3.2 billion in March, HPS for $8.5 billion in stock consideration in July, and ElmTree real estate firm in September, adding $165 billion in client AUM. Recorded significant indefinite-lived and finite-lived management contracts plus investor relationships from HPS transaction. Issued €1.0 billion notes due 2035, repaid €700 million notes at maturity, and contributed Circle stake to BlackRock Charitable Fund.
macro_exposures: Business remains exposed to market volatility impacts on $7.8 trillion equity AUM and fee generation, with particular sensitivity to equity market movements. AUM decreased $166 billion from foreign exchange movements as US dollar strengthened against major currencies, though approximately 80% of revenue generated in US dollars provides partial natural hedge.
forward_outlook: Management targets raising $400 billion in private markets by 2030 supported by integrated public-private portfolio capabilities and plans to launch first LifePath target date fund with private markets in 2026. Technology platform integration of Aladdin, eFront, and Preqin expected to enhance whole-portfolio solutions and drive continued organic growth. Board authorized additional share repurchase program while maintaining strong liquidity position.
uncertainty_profile: Contingent consideration liabilities of $8.4 billion subject to performance metrics and post-closing conditions for GIP and HPS transactions over approximately five years. Integration risks across three major acquisitions could impact expected synergies and client retention. Unrecognized tax benefits with potential effective tax rate impact, while deferred carried interest liability increased with uncertain recognition timing.
